claims.statement: Gravity-related collapse remains exploratory until gravitational self-energy of a superposed mass distribution predicts a decoherence or collapse timescale that is independently testable.

constraints: collapse scale must depend on mass distribution
the claim must be falsifiable against decoherence bounds
the node may not be used to certify consciousness or mature time-crystal order

sources.citation: Penrose, On Gravity's Role in Quantum State Reduction, DOI 10.1007/BF02105068
Diosi, A universal master equation for the gravitational violation of quantum mechanics, DOI 10.1016/0375-9601(87)90681-5
Donadi et al., Underground test of gravity-related wave function collapse, DOI 10.1038/s41567-020-1008-4

claims.statement: The Diosi-Penrose timescale `tau ~ hbar / E_G` is only a diagnostic estimate for gravity-related collapse and must remain tied to measurable bounds.

constraints: the timescale must be computed from a defined gravitational self-energy difference
the estimate must remain benchmarked against observed decoherence
the node must not be promoted to a certified collapse law

sources.citation: Penrose, On Gravity's Role in Quantum State Reduction, DOI 10.1007/BF02105068
Diosi, A universal master equation for the gravitational violation of quantum mechanics, DOI 10.1016/0375-9601(87)90681-5

claims.statement: Gravitational time-dilation decoherence is an exploratory benchmark in which proper-time differences produce measurable phase dispersion or decoherence in composite states.

constraints: the effect must be measured as proper-time-dependent phase loss
the result must remain benchmark-level rather than consciousness-level
the node must not replace the many-body Hamiltonian

sources.citation: Pikovski et al., Universal decoherence due to gravitational time dilation, DOI 10.1038/nphys3366
Carlesso et al., collapse bounds and related experiments, Phys. Rev. D 94, 124036

claims.statement: Microtubule mesoscopic observables remain exploratory transport and perturbation measurements and do not establish Orch-OR or time-crystal order by themselves.

constraints: observables must be reproducible
the lane must not imply consciousness by observation alone
the measurements must stay separate from mature stellar or granular diagnostics

sources.citation: Hameroff and Penrose, Consciousness in the universe: a review of the Orch OR theory, DOI 10.1016/j.plrev.2013.08.002
Tegmark, The importance of quantum decoherence in brain processes, DOI 10.1103/PhysRevE.61.4194
Hagan, Hameroff, Tuszynski, Quantum computation in brain microtubules: decoherence and biological feasibility, DOI 10.1103/PhysRevE.65.061901

claims.statement: Microtubule vibrational modes are only admissible as exploratory observables when reproducible spectral peaks, linewidths, and quality factors are measured.

constraints: a mode must be reproducibly measured
the mode must survive controls and noise accounting
the node cannot be used as proof of consciousness or time-crystal order

sources.citation: Vibrations of microtubules: Physics that has not met biology yet, DOI 10.1016/j.wavemoti.2016.12.006
Kalra et al., Electronic Energy Migration in Microtubules, DOI 10.1021/acscentsci.2c01114

claims.statement: Microtubule energy migration is an exploratory transport observable requiring measurable transport length, anisotropy, damping, and temperature dependence.

constraints: transport length must exceed the noise floor
anisotropy must be reproducible
the effect must remain distinct from generic thermal diffusion

sources.citation: Kalra et al., Electronic Energy Migration in Microtubules, DOI 10.1021/acscentsci.2c01114
Engel et al., Evidence for wavelike energy transfer through quantum coherence in photosynthetic systems, DOI 10.1038/nature05678

claims.statement: Anesthetic perturbation is an exploratory probe of microtubule observables and must not be promoted to a consciousness proof.

constraints: observable shifts must track binding or dose
the perturbation must be measurable in transport or vibration channels
the claim must not be inflated into Orch-OR evidence

sources.citation: Craddock et al., anesthetic-tubulin modeling and related perturbation work, DOI 10.1080/07391102.2021.1897044
Hameroff and Penrose, Orch OR review, DOI 10.1016/j.plrev.2013.08.002

claims.statement: A driven-dissipative time crystal is only admissible when subharmonic order and a finite robustness window are both measured.

constraints: subharmonic response must be measured
the system must remain open or driven-dissipative
no-go boundaries must be respected

sources.citation: Zhang et al., Observation of a discrete time crystal, DOI 10.1038/nature21413
Choi et al., Observation of discrete time-crystalline order in a disordered dipolar many-body system, DOI 10.1038/nature21426
Kessler et al., Observation of a Dissipative Time Crystal, DOI 10.1103/PhysRevLett.127.043602

claims.statement: Subharmonic locking is the minimal time-crystal observable: the response period must be an integer multiple of the drive period and remain stable under perturbation.

constraints: the response must show period multiplication
the lock must survive repeated runs
periodicity alone is not sufficient

sources.citation: Zhang et al., Observation of a discrete time crystal, DOI 10.1038/nature21413
Kessler et al., Observation of a Dissipative Time Crystal, DOI 10.1103/PhysRevLett.127.043602

claims.statement: Time-crystal order requires a finite robustness window against disorder, dissipation, and drive changes.

constraints: small perturbations must not destroy the response
the window must be finite and reproducible
the claim must remain distinct from transient resonance

sources.citation: Choi et al., Observation of discrete time-crystalline order in a disordered dipolar many-body system, DOI 10.1038/nature21426
Kessler et al., Observation of a Dissipative Time Crystal, DOI 10.1103/PhysRevLett.127.043602

claims.statement: Time-crystal claims must respect published no-go boundaries and cannot rely on periodicity alone.

constraints: equilibrium or finite-size no-go results must be checked
analogies do not bypass the no-go boundary
the lane must fail when the no-go conditions are violated

sources.citation: Bruno, impossibility of spontaneously rotating time crystals, DOI 10.1007/JHEP10(2015)001
Zhang et al., Observation of a discrete time crystal, DOI 10.1038/nature21413

claims.statement: Room-temperature quantum coherence is treated as a benchmark problem before any microtubule claim is promoted.

constraints: the benchmark must compare coherence time and damping against known room-temperature examples
the lane must not treat microtubule claims as already validated
the benchmark may remain negative

sources.citation: Engel et al., Evidence for wavelike energy transfer through quantum coherence in photosynthetic systems, DOI 10.1038/nature05678
Kalra et al., Electronic Energy Migration in Microtubules, DOI 10.1021/acscentsci.2c01114

claims.statement: Triplet-band resonance is an exploratory observable and only counts when the triplet pattern is directly measured and independently reproduced.

constraints: repeatable triplet bands must be measured
band spacing must survive independent calibration
the observable cannot be promoted into a geometry law without a geometry observable

sources.citation: Saxena et al., scale-free resonant multiscale behavior, DOI 10.3390/fractalfract4020011
Saxena et al., polyatomic time crystal report, DOI 10.1063/5.0130618
Ghosh et al., coherence and resonance hierarchy work, DOI 10.1080/19420889.2022.2071101

claims.statement: Scale-free resonance hierarchy remains an exploratory hypothesis until nested frequency clusters obey a reproducible scaling law.

constraints: the scaling exponent must be reproducible
the hierarchy must survive control analysis
the hypothesis must stay separate from mature stellar observables

sources.citation: Saxena et al., Scale-free resonant multiscale behavior, DOI 10.3390/fractalfract4020011
Saxena et al., polyatomic time crystal report, DOI 10.1063/5.0130618

claims.statement: The polyatomic time crystal is only a hypothesis until subharmonic order and robustness are measured in a coupled resonant system.

constraints: subharmonic locking must be observed
robustness must be measured
the claim must not be confused with a certified biological time crystal

sources.citation: Saxena et al., polyatomic time crystal report, DOI 10.1063/5.0130618
Zhang et al., Observation of a discrete time crystal, DOI 10.1038/nature21413
Kessler et al., Observation of a Dissipative Time Crystal, DOI 10.1103/PhysRevLett.127.043602

claims.statement: The triplets-of-triplets pattern is an exploratory nested-resonance hypothesis and requires independent reproduction across scale.

constraints: triplet nesting must be reproducible
instrument artifacts must be excluded
the hypothesis must not be used as a mature geometry claim

sources.citation: Ghosh et al., coherence and resonance hierarchy work, DOI 10.1080/19420889.2022.2071101
Saxena et al., Scale-free resonant multiscale behavior, DOI 10.3390/fractalfract4020011
Saxena et al., polyatomic time crystal report, DOI 10.1063/5.0130618

claims.statement: The spacetime-triplet-geometry idea remains speculative until a direct geometry observable exists and the triplet structure survives comparison against collapse, time-crystal, and microtubule benchmarks.

constraints: a direct geometry observable must be defined
the pattern must survive cross-checks against collapse and time-crystal bounds
the hypothesis may not be used to certify a mature spacetime law

Diosi, A universal master equation for the gravitational violation of quantum mechanics, DOI 10.1016/0375-9601(87)90681-5
